title: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact
display_title: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact
wikitext:
[]: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact?             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:41 am Why do you think they wanted to initite 3i? Was it because they each had some sort of insecurity?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:48 am Misato tells us why. Its because they wanted mankind to go past the stage of evolution we had reached. You also figure out why in EOTV. Its because Instrumentality/3I was supposed to fill in the gaps or voids people felt, that we all were lacking something and are afraid of it. I would elaborate more, but I need to watch EoTV again as well as EOE.

[]: Re: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact?             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Sep 03, 2009 2:49 am Shinji-kun wrote: Why do you think they wanted to initite 3i? Was it because they each had some sort of insecurity?

[]: Re: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact?             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Thu Sep 03, 2009 9:09 am Allemann wrote: Shinji-kun wrote: Why do you think they wanted to initite 3i? Was it because they each had some sort of insecurity?

[]: Re: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact?             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Sep 03, 2009 10:32 am Shinji-kun wrote: Allemann wrote: Shinji-kun wrote: Why do you think they wanted to initite 3i? Was it because they each had some sort of insecurity?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Thu Sep 03, 2009 11:45 am Seele wanted to start the next stage of evolution. Their goal was to reach the Omega Point, where all humans are united as one god-like being. There would be no pain, suffering, loneliness, or even any concept of the "self", and all of the human insecurities and doubts would be finaly eliminated.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Sep 03, 2009 11:55 am Holy Diver wrote: Seele wanted to start the next stage of evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:02 pm Allemann wrote: Holy Diver wrote: Seele wanted to start the next stage of evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:08 pm Holy Diver wrote: And that's why Instrumentality failed, because it was an abomination that, given the selfish nature of man, was doomed from the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:35 pm I think the question being asked here is, "Why did SEELE think that Instrumentality was necessary. They gave us their alleged reasons, but how did they reach their conclusions?"

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:52 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I think the question being asked here is, "Why did SEELE think that Instrumentality was necessary. They gave us their alleged reasons, but how did they reach their conclusions?"

[]: Re: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact?             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu Sep 03, 2009 1:02 pm Shinji-kun wrote: Why do you think they wanted to initiate 3i?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Thu Sep 03, 2009 4:09 pm Allemann wrote: Shinji was selfish?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Sep 03, 2009 4:29 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I think the question being asked here is, "Why did SEELE think that Instrumentality was necessary. They gave us their alleged reasons, but how did they reach their conclusions?"

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Sep 03, 2009 4:31 pm Holy Diver wrote: In a way, yes. He chose to experience real feelings, rather than the potential happiness of instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Sep 03, 2009 9:00 pm There’s no such thing as false happiness. Whatever makes you happy is true happiness, regardless of how others might see it. As for instrumentality, the only problem I see with it is that people were given no chance to decide for themselves if they wanted to take part in it or to continue go their own way. As far as I’m concerned, people can pursue any form of happiness they choose, wither that means continuing to live their lives as they always have or going off and becoming part of some giant cosmic being. As long as it’s their personal choice, more power to them.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Sep 04, 2009 9:10 am child of Lilith wrote: There’s no such thing as false happiness.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Sep 04, 2009 10:43 am I've always found it appropriate that the 'evolution' of Instrumentality is actually a de-evolution. It's not an advancement to a greater stage but a return to Genesis in Lilith's womb. It certainly matches the themes of NGE. Again we have brilliant Anno associating that concept with paradise/Eden and 'one with god'. As for why they REALLY wanted it; Seele strikes me as the type of people who are the most cut-off from humanity. Jung said that if individuals ever did reach godhood that state would be filled with the loneliest individuals because gods can't be one with others. Seele have certainly set themselves up in that manner. Of course, we don't know what they do when they're not all conspiring in the evil lair with neon lights, but they don't strike me as very socially happy individuals. Even though we aren't told in the series, I'd say what drives them is the same as what drives every character to make contact with others; loneliness. @false happiness: Since happiness is an abstract term, the idea of what's false and true happiness is equally abstract and entirely subjective. If drugs provide happiness, why is that happiness false? I'm sure scientists would probably tell you what drugs do to us chemically is simply what would happen to us naturally when we have certain 'happy' moments in life (it's just on a larger, manipulated scale).

[]: Re: Why did SEELE want to initiate 3rd impact?             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Sep 04, 2009 10:53 am Shinji-kun wrote: Why do you think they wanted to initite 3i? Was it because they each had some sort of insecurity?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Sep 04, 2009 12:41 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I've always found it appropriate that the 'evolution' of Instrumentality is actually a de-evolution. It's not an advancement to a greater stage but a return to Genesis in Lilith's womb.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:48 am Misato tells us why. Its because they wanted mankind to go past the stage of evolution we had
